notes: Dashboard clients should send X-School-ID when the authenticated user has more than one school membership.
Existing endpoints are included as implemented routes; document/correspondence endpoints are the target dashboard contract for the new administrative archive module.

Authorization.description: JWT access token returned by login.

X-School-ID.description: School context. Defaults to primary active membership when omitted.

X-Request-ID.description: Client supplied tracing id. Server may also generate one.

Idempotency-Key.required: for sensitive POST/PATCH actions marked idempotent
Idempotency-Key.description: Used for safe retries of state-changing sensitive operations.

success.notes: If a legacy endpoint currently returns raw DRF data, dashboard should normalize through the API client until the endpoint is migrated to the unified envelope.

response.body: Same as GET /api/v1/admin/documents with needs_reply=true and status not REPLIED/CLOSED/ARCHIVED

implementation_notes: Principal or school manager roles should receive all document permissions inside their own school.
Document numbers must be unique inside a school; generated sequence numbers must be transaction-safe per school/year/direction.
PDF attachments must be stored with FileField-backed media storage, never inside the database.
Central IT should not receive direct access to school correspondence unless explicitly delegated through school context and RBAC.
All sensitive actions must write both DocumentActivityLog and the shared AuditLog when available.
